Let me know if something here is wrong, you think I should add something, or if you have questions

**New 3.1 stuff is down at the bottom**  
This is the example character I will be using (until we get past the 7 basic steps anyway)  
\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`  
I am going to go in order from left to right, the section discussed will be bolded

## Basics

### Step 1: the base

*\`**statue**.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
This makes the custom hero have the base of statue  
The main reason you may want to change a base is because you can get abilities from other characters. For example, if I changed the base to Pockets, then it would give Pockets ability to my custom character  
Same thing goes for spells, having a base of Mage will have a character who knows ‘Poke’ (though this can be easily replicated and I will show how later)

Theres also a couple other minor things the base affects

* if you do not include the .sd shown later, the default sides will be the same as the base  
* this is where barks come from. For example if your base is Ludus, the character will say “lock it” when they roll their highest pip side  
* The base determines how idols and the alliteration keyword interact, they are based on the base hero name, not the name you give your custom hero. So if you have a hero named Bob but the base was warden, the alliteration keyword will proc on W and the Idol of Aiiu will give you 2 hp not 1

To make our base statue, we start with by just writing statue  
	*\`**statue**\`*

### Step 2: color

*\`statue.**col.o**.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
This sets the color of the custom hero  
IMPORTANT: bases cannot be set to the same color. If I tried to set the color to n (green, which is statues default color), for example, it would give me an error. In this case you just don’t include col.\# at all  
List of all available colors, paste it into a custom to take a look:  
*Party.fey.col.a.n.a+fey.col.b.n.b+fey.col.c.n.c+fey.col.d.n.d+fey.col.e.n.e+fey.col.g.n.g+fey.col.h.n.h+fey.col.i.n.i+fey.col.k.n.k+fey.col.l.n.l+fey.col.m.n.m+fey.col.n.n.n+fey.col.o.n.o+fey.col.p.n.p+fey.col.q.n.q+fey.n.r+fey.col.s.n.s+fey.col.t.n.t+fey.col.u.n.u+fey.col.v.n.v+fey.col.w.n.w+fey.col.x.n.x+fey.col.y.n.y+fey.col.z.n.z*  
(I dont recommend using vuish or muish, they dont really work)

So for our example, we want our character to be orange so we add .col.o  
*\`statue.**col.o**\`*

### Step 3: hp

*\`statue.col.o.**hp.6**.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
Very simple, whatever the number is, is how much max hp the custom hero will have  
To give our hero 6 max hp we add .hp.6  
*\`statue.col.o.**hp.6**\`*

### Step 4: tier

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.**tier.2**.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
Again really simple, whatever number you put is the tier of hero it will be.  
To make our hero a tier 2 we add .tier.2  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.**tier.2**\`*

### Step 5: name

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.**n.Punpun Is No Funfun**.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
Whatever you put after n. will the be the name of the character  
If I wanted to name this character bob instead I would put  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.**n.Bob**.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0\`*  
Let’s just go with my username and name it Punpun Is No Funfun  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.**n.Punpun Is No Funfun**\`*  
You’ll notice the text now scrolls, this happens with names that are longer. Usually you dont want this as theres some strange graphical glitches like black boxes apprearing. But this is just an example so we’ll just go with it

### Step 6: sides

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun**.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0**\`*  
.sd. uses a big list of side ids to determine what sides a character has, where, and what pips (if any) those sides are. Luckily for you someone made a handy tool to make this much easier  
Make a copy of [this](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1GKdxQNPNXJ1vL4EzDKVdDmL1qY2Q3rF4k95iFII90sk/edit#gid=0)  
Now simply select the side you want to replace and type, you should see a list appear, click on the one that is what you want and then enter the pip value you want

*“But I want vulnerable pristine 1 dmg to all\!”*  
The sd. section is only for base sides that exist in slice and dice. You can get your 1 damage to all here but thats it. See “giving keywords to sides” to add more keywords to a base side

If you want to see what base sides exist, see page 2 of that sheet or use the graph in the ledger of Slice and Dice  
Base sides will have their keywords already applied btw, if you use damage poison the side will already come with the poison keyword.  
Now as an example, lets fill out our character

1. For the left side I want 1 damage  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type damage, then I click on ‘15:Damage’ when it shows up  
   2. Next I type ‘1’ in the pips section below that

***(Below just repeats the process 4 more times with different sides, only difference is \#6)***

2. For the center I want 1 shield  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type shield, then I click on ‘56:Shield’ when it shows up  
   2. Next I type ‘1’ in the pips section below that  
3. For the top I want 1 damage poison  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type shield, then I click on ‘53:Damage Poison’ when it shows up  
   2. Next I type ‘1’ in the pips section below that  
4. For the bottom I want 1 damage deathwish  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type deathwish, then I click on ‘20:Damage Deathwish when it shows up  
   2. Next I type ‘1’ in the pips section below that  
5. For the right I want 1 damage cantrip  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type cantrip, then I click on ‘126:Damage Cantrip when it shows up  
   2. Next I type ‘1’ in the pips section below that  
6. For the rightmost I want a target ally side  
   1. I click on the ‘select’ section below left and start to type ally, then I click on ‘176:Target Ally’ when it shows up  
   2. I don’t need the pips for this side because it has none

Finally we take the .sd that was generated and paste it at the end  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun**.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0**\`*

### Step 7: portrait (recommended to do last)

The portrait will be the same as your base if you don’t change this

For making your own, hero portraits seem to be 25x25 pixels (a little less usually but that’s the largest)  
So simply pull up your program of choice, make it 25x25 and draw a portrait  
Then upload it to [https://tann.fun/things/dice-img](https://tann.fun/things/dice-img) and get the string  
Then take the string and plug it at the end of the character (it can go anywhere but they tend to be large so I prefer them at the end) by putting .img.(string here)  
For example  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0**.img.3525rw269w28652988389992o3000=ZZ111=%zOxhxMxjydxmwcxnxaxjwjx0Gjyjw2x5kw4yuw7xsw8996sw7wtw7wu**\`*

There are also some base game ones, for example: .img.goblin will replace the portrait with a goblin  
You can also modify this base game image with a bunch of other stuff, which you can find in the Texture section in Textmod \-\> Api  
Its a lot and admittedly I dont understand most of it, I just guess values till I get the color changes I want when I do use base game images

# Slightly More Advanced stuff

If all you wanted to know was how to put base sides on a hero, name it, give it hp, and give it a new portrait, you’re done btw

## Giving Keywords to sides

Let's put vulnerable pristine on our left side and let’s also add the keywords share and ranged to the rightmost target ally side  
We can do that with i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.ranged  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.**i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.ranged**\`*  
Now let’s deconstruct that:

### .i

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0**.i**.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine**.i**.rightmost.k.share\#k.ranged\`*  
We give keywords to characters using items, .i. Is what you use to apply an item to a character  
This works with any item, like if you wanted to give a character \+5 empty max hp, you could use .i.scar

### .(side)

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.i**.left**.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine.i**.rightmost**.k.share\#k.ranged\`*  
These make it so the following items only apply to those specific sides, if you didn’t include them, then in the above example all sides would have vulnerable, pristine, ranged, and share keywords

Left: left  
Middle: mid  
Top: top  
Bottom: bot  
Right: Right  
Rightmost: rightmost

There are also special combined sides for stuff like top AND bottom, here’s the list:  
*all, left2, mid2, right2, right 3, row, col, topbot, right 5*

### .k.(keyword)

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.i.left.**k.vulnerable**\#**k.pristine**.i.rightmost.**k.share**\#**k.ranged**\`*  
Allows you to add keywords, simply replace (keyword) with what you want  
Some keywords cannot apply to all sides however, in the above example our rightmost side isn’t actually getting the ranged keyword  
We can fix this with the permissive keyword like so  
*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.*i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine*.i.rightmost.k.share\#**k.permissive**\#k.ranged\`*  
Some keywords do have to be in order btw, if it was ranged then permissive, it wouldn’t work

#### \#

This chains items together, saves you from having to write out i.rightmost or i.left over and over, and simply acts as a “and” between your k.(keyword)s

## Giving Base Game spells or tactics to characters

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.*i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine*.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.permissive\#k.ranged**.i.learn.flare**\`*  
All you do is just add \`.i.learn.(spell/tactic here)’ somewhere in the character

To give out character Flare, we add .i.learn.flare

You can also make your own custom spells, see below if you are interested

## Stealing traits

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.*i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine*.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.permissive\#k.ranged.i.learn.flare.**i.t.gnoll**\`*  
You can steal traits off monsters or (some) heroes with i.t.(creature)

This is NOT shown in level up screens\! Players will be unaware this character has gnolls trait until they pick them and it is displayed when clicked on

If we wanted to steal gnolls \-1 incoming damage ability we just put i.t.gnoll

## Adding Curses / Blessings to hero

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.*i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine*.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.permissive\#k.ranged.i.learn.flare.i.t.gnoll**.i.self.deep pockets’***  
Use .self.(curse or blessing name here) to apply said curse or blessing to the hero  
Some curses and blessings will not work  
This is NOT shown in level up screens\! Players will be unaware this character has \+1 item slot until they actually take them and see for themselves

For our example, let’s add Deep Pockets (+1 item slot) by writing .i.self.deep.pockets

## Jinx effects on heroes

*\`statue.col.o.hp.6.tier.2.n.Punpun Is No Funfun.sd.15-1:56-1:53-1:20-1:126-1:176-0.*i.left.k.vulnerable\#k.pristine*.i.rightmost.k.share\#k.permissive\#k.ranged.i.learn.flare.i.t.gnoll.i.self.deep pockets.i.t.jinx.hero.focus\`*  
You can use .t.jinx to apply curses/blessings to all heroes  
Again, these are NOT shown in level up screens

For our example, let’s add Hero.Focus to all heroes while this character is alive by writing .i.t.jinx.hero.focus

Some curses/blessings can be finicky, try using allitem.self.(curse/blessing) or t.unpack.jinx.(curse/blessing) if it doesnt work

# Adding Custom Spells

### Add \`.abilitydata.()’

To give a hero a custom spell start by adding .abilitydata()  
I highly recommend using parentheses. You will enter everything between them  
Strangely, it seems best to put this AFTER your .img.  
For example, *\`thief.img.goblin.abilitydata.(spell)\`*

### Creating the ‘spell hero’

Next you need to make a character that the spell will be based upon, see above to learn how to do that

For this example, we will use the following as our spell and be altering it as we go  
*\`statue.sd.15-1:0-0:0-0:0-0:76-1:0-0\`*  
And we will just be using thief to add the spell to, but you can use literally anyone, including custom heroes  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(spell data here)\`*

If you want to add the custom spell as an ITEM instead of just being prebuilt onto a hero, you just use i.t.(hero ***with*** the custom spell)  
For example, if we wanted the spell to be an item we would put  
*\`i.t.Thief.abilitydata.(spell data here)\`*

### Targeted ability/main effect (left side)

This is the leftmost side, whatever is listed here is what will happen when the spell is cast. It also changes how the spell is targeted, having a shield here instead of damage will make it target allies

If we want it to deal 2 damage to all poison, we just replace the sd with the following  
\`sd.54-2:0-0:0-0:0-0:76-1:0-0\`  
Which results in:  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(statue.sd.54-2:0-0:0-0:0-0:76-1:0-0)\`*

### Extra effects (mid side)

This is the middle side, whatever is listed here is the extra effect when cast  
Like if we wanted our 2 dmg to all poison effect to also shield the team we could add shield 1 to all as the middle side by changing the sd to sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-1:0-0  
Which results in:  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(statue.sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-1:0-0)\`*  
Importantly, these cannot be targeted sides. It has to be stuff like ‘1 damage to all’ or ‘Target all 1 heal’

### Mana Cost (right side)

This is the right side, whatever is listed here will be the manacost  
If we wanted to increase the cost of this spell we just alter the pip value of the right side, let’s make it 4 cost  
All we do is alter the sd to sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-4:0-0  
Which results in:  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(statue.sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-4:0-0)\`*  
(You dont have to use a mana side for this, I just like to)

### Extras (image, keywords, name)

Just like creating a custom hero, you can also add images and keywords to the sides you want the spell to use. It’s the exact same steps as making a custom hero, all this does after all is base a spell off a hero  
For example if I wanted the picture to be my pfp and the name of the spell to be ‘test’ and to give the 2 damage poison to all the vulnerable keyword  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(statue.sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-0:0-0.n.test.i.left.k.vulnerable.img.3525rw269w28652988389992o3000=ZZ111=%zOxhxMxjydxmwcxnxaxjwjx0Gjyjw2x5kw4yuw7xsw8996sw7wtw7wu)\`*

#### **Keywords that work**

Most keywords will not work, here is a list of all keywords that work with spells  
![][image1]

### Spell Keywords

Below is a list of keywords that cant normally be applied to sides  
To apply them to your sides, just use i.(ritemx here)  
For example, if I also wanted to add channel I would add the ritemx.302ea5e.part.0 to all sides  
*\`Thief.abilitydata.(statue.sd.54-2:72-1:0-0:0-0:76-3:0-0.n.test.i.ritemx.302ea5e.part.0.i.left.k.vulnerable)\`*

#### List of spell keywords

**Channel**   
ritemx.302ea5e.part.0

**Deplete**  
ritemx.539ce9a

**singleCast**  
Ritemx.132fb.part.1

**spellRescue**  
Ritemx.62e8

**cooldown**  
Ritemx.161bf

**future**   
Unpack.ritemx.644f

# Cool Advanced stuff I’ve learned while making heroes

This isn’t really gonna be sorted in any way, but heres a bunch of neat things I’ve found. Each of these will have an example hero at the top  
I’ll update as I go and find more neat stuff out

### .doc on heroes

*\`(statue.hp.4.tier.1.col.b.n.Flux.sd.77-1:77-1:93-2:93-2:187-1:187-1.i.right2.k.managain\#k.fault\#k.cantrip.i.right.k.singleuse.i.topbot.k.shifter.img.342r802F8C3Jr2D3LF482ij5aa0000kmRHC333tffIso0ov0vE247dklBBB0CK0nwQGAmmmSojzEHh68U0Y0V0Wfehg5SXfeg4D4QYgeg199030Xg4g0rl9r2Ze0rpy091Yeq0k=k9140We0k=79C404eq06bc7c9D5e0edc=J4e86caiadaJ5saiumidmJ4kscumibci03D40xbAac6a03L==cQFe8c=7caQLa7cbcaRLic6bcaTF8aic7aUF8ibaV**.doc.right 2 is basically \-1 mana (can go negative)\[n\] \[n\]by punpun**.abilitydata.(statue.sd.88-3:0-0:0-0:0-0:76-2:0-0.i.blindfold.i.k.heavy\#k.eliminate.n.Wild Magic.img.Cursed Bolt.hsv.-10:30:0.b.fff))\`*  
This can be used to create a description to inform the player of things, sadly this is not shown in level up screens  
I plan on writing a richtext guide eventually, ill link it here when I do

### Side replacement on specific turns

*statue.col.y.hp.7.tier.2.n.Wererat.sd.175-2:90-2:115-2:115-2:53-1:0-0.doc.Sides become \[rat\] on every even turn**.i.self.(et2.allitem.(hat.(thief.sd.170-3:170-3:170-2:170-2:170-2:170-2.n.rats sides)).n.rats sides)**.img.362l0e2n992z202Dac3HWgX3Jl2m2jj247cdejj8000QGAtffIso7nqqhejfuuoei7k88BBBbsxpsv78adgd9ab=753eQe==652geZ3=6Hh2f3%HhE27HhF262X25h1wd8l63525h12D93e34e24gk0s3c98cl3025k0si2d98cl2e34k1i0Da98a0f02mkzi02c0a9z2eJ41ca212ca121251cCrb0u7Jzd8a0quD0y7l30bdn9ca7e2Aizbcn9b7e2eizaoDnca%f0zob0D9b=eJ4oao0ada=6*  
You can use i.self.et\#.allitem.(hat.thief.(side id) to make sides chage on intervals, in this example every 2nd turn they’re sides become rats sides  
If you used et3 instead, it would change every 3rd turn  
If you use t2 instead, it would only change on turn 2

### Summoning

*\`(statue.hp.6.tier.3.col.b.sd.18-1:18-1:90-3:90-3:18-1:160-0.i.left2.k.tall.i.right.k.tall\#k.singleuse.**i.t.jinx.summon.barrel**.n.Conjurer.img..i.learn.drop.doc.by punpun)\`*  
It seems like everyones first hero idea was to make a summoner, sadly this is much harder than you’d think  
Sides can only summon enemies, and only bones (via boned/hyper boned) or dragon (with the dragon egg side)  
Spells can only summon dragon at the moment, and hes still an enemy  
The only real way to do summoning at the moment is to use jinx to summon an enemy that benefits you, in this case, Conjurer summons barrels that can be used to cascade damage downward to nuke a boss if enough show up  
These hero.i.t.jinx.summon.(monsters) CANNOT have items built it, which limits summoning further. Like I couldn’t make Conjurer summon \`barrel.i.scar\`

### Future on sides

*\`(statue.hp.8.tier.3.n.Foresight.col.b.sd.82-4:124-0:82-3:82-3:123-0:123-0**.i.right3.(unpack.ritemx.644f)**.img.332r%22xa43zaec2ij000ABAmmmINI96acbeb8deeeqifM6Cjcct7mIso%jQ=KRh68h9dBokd20FbbaKafXeaHz4SeaFzQ7QfDzRrimfCacRrq3m81a82Wrq3jm18Y72si3jem0bWrqR4em0b5rsiQein0b4273qi2sj4n9827Qjs365e947242qQ7998x624R79l808x25379khkw08a5x79kgpuku80xx248kgpogl080b04c1gyogvg081a041d0gyoh18Cd1ckw0ch18Ed4cgkc0A18Gc50kc1c8E.doc.by punpun.abilitydata.(statue.sd.178-1:182-2:0-0:0-0:76-2:0-0.i.k.damage.n.Calamity.img.pray.hsv.-10:10:20))\`*  
Afaik, this is the only spell keyword that actually works on sides  
And even so, its very janky. Some keywords like pain, poison, or cleave just wont work on these sides. Generally tho, it works exactly how you’d think  
You can make a bunch of interesting stuff with it, in the example above, the character uses future dodge to negate the pain from their mana sides and damage from the spell on the next turn

### Deplete / Channel on 2 spells

*\`statue.col.r.hp.10.tier.3.n.Surg3on.sd.61-2:61-2:105-4:105-4:109-2:109-2.img.2oo000YVJFwqCok777mmmquxqlkb8dhhhcbefdd247cccpeeodiAtnNEOBxvnnndbcyliffgmbgNkadbhoTMqadahlkga3SqddblgkaVaPqadcbkbaocWPkcbbadloUc3Pakgkadakow4Ue3eOakokdao5xcW4iNakgbaZ4w4V5eNkogkoY7eF83im9NkgpZ4im6Ge6n78Noh6Z=G4987eNuIhgZ46iwK8u8%N8im6hY6i8v8vi7Nie6jhg68A8A8A9mjNie7i8gy99998nOqe%9c39998mPif7escyXdgPeie7emgyXcakPiei7emhyVbkmPqfsjm5cUycaiRqftqYc3dsiSqjreZ46esiTNse6Y6esmTNqteDCsmR**.abilitydata.(statue.sd.136-1:0-0:0-0:0-0:76-2:0-0.n.Botched Surgery.i.unpack.ritemx.644f.img.first aid kit.hsv.0:-50:0).abilitydata.(statue.sd.177-999:0:0:0:76-0:0.i.k.damage.i.ritemx.539ce9a.n.Botched Surgery.img.2a8000defob3DBuHbbxkc=jjYVJ7h6gE85gE85gE85gE85go8o48o8ow4o8Mw4px4UxM5)\`***  
If you have 2 spells named the same thing on the same character, using one spell will deplete / channel the both  
Above is an example from my Her0 M0de, when you use the knife version of the spell, both version will deplete

# 3.1 stuff 

I will eventually integrate this with the rest of the guide but for now while i actually write it down itll be in this this section here, and i assume most people visiting this guide again are here for this

# Custom Tactics

The steps here are the same as creating custom spells, but instead of using the right side as the manacost, you use the top, bottom, and rightmost sides for the tactic cost

For example thief.abilitydata.mage will give you a tactic that gives you 2 mana, then 2 mana, and costs 0

To create a custom tactic, leave the right side blank. Use one of the following in the top, bottom, and/or rightmost side of the dice to set the cost

* Damage is damage pips cost  
  * Heal is heal pips cost  
  * Shield is shield pips cost  
  * Revive is any pip cost  
  * Unset blank is blank cost  
  * 1 keyword: any side not listed above with 1 keyword...  
  * 2 keyword: see above  
  * "1 pip cost": any side not listed above with (not 1, 2, or 4\) keywords and 1 pip  
  * "2 pip cost": any side not listed above with (not 1, 2, or 4\) keywords and 2 pips  
  * "3 pip cost": see above  
  * "4 pip cost": see above  
  * 4 keyword: see above above

For example, a tactic that does 2 ranged damage and costs 2 damage pips, is called Hurl, and uses a facade as a sprite:  
thief.abilitydata.(mage.sd.46-2:0-0:15-2.n.Hurl.img.hinder.hsv.0:40:-45.draw.Spi101:-2:-3)

# Monsterpool

To add custom monsters to the game you use  
monsterpool.(monster)  
For example, replacing all monsters with a rat named test:  
	Monsterpool.(rat.n.Test)

That will replace ALL monsters with our Test rat, if you do not want to replace monsters, instead put .part.1 at the end, for example, adding a rat named test without overwriting  
	Monsterpool.(rat.n.Test).part.1

If you use .part.0 instead, all that will happen is you clear all monsters, regardless of what monster is in the pool. This can be nice if you want an ‘rmons only’ mode or simply wish to clear *then* add. For example  
	Monsterpool.rat.part.0

### Zones

Unless specified, monsterpool will apply to ALL floors, including bosses. If you add things to boss floors it will mess with the boss generation. Because of that you generally only want to apply a new monster from certain floors. You can do this with  
	(number here)-(number here).monsterpool.(rat.n.Test).part.1  
For example  
	1-2.monsterpool.(rat.n.Test).part.1  
Will add our test rat as an enemy the game can show from floors 1 to 2

 I’d recommend doing this using the zones that already exist in game. Below is an image of each zone and the enemies that spawn in it. Sorted by Size then by A-Z  
![][image2]  
Floors 1-3 is Forest  
Floors 5-7 is Dungeon  
Floors 9-11 is Catacombs  
Floors 13-15 is Lair  
Floors 17-19 is Pit

So to add our rat named Test to just forest and lair zones, we would do  
	1-3.Monsterpool.(rat.n.Test).part.1  
	13-15.Monsterpool.(rat.n.Test).part.1

#### Zones Pastes

Here are some quick pastes if you would like to replace a monster from the normal zones. Use the clear all paste then the modified zones you want

Please note that rarity seems to not be taken into account when ‘adding’ monsters. I saw like 3 z0mbies (0.02 rarity) and a handful of grandmas (0.1 rarity) in testing

Clear all monsterpool except bosses:  
1-3.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&5-7.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&9-11.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&13-15.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&17-19.monsterpool.Bee.part.0.mn.Clear Monsterpool  
Forest:  
1-3.monsterpool.Archer+Bee+Chest+Log+Rat+Seed+Thorn+Goblin+Grandma+Wolf+Boar.part.1.mn.Forest.doc.Floors 1 thru 3  
Dungeon:  
5-7.monsterpool.Chest+Illusion+Shade+Bandit+Blind+Barrel+Dragon Egg+Fountain+Goblin+Golem+Militia+Quartz+Snake+Sudul+Warchief+Cyclops+Ogre+Slate+Slimer.part.1.mn.Dungeon.doc.Floors 5 thru 7  
Catacombs:  
9-11.monsterpool.Bones+Chest+Grave+Illusion+Imp+Shade+Sniper+Carrier+Fanatic+Fountain+Golem+Militia+Quartz+Sudul+Z0mbie+Zombie+Banshee+Ghost+Slimer+Spiker+Wizz.part.1.mn.Catacombs.doc.Floors 9 thru 11  
Lair:  
13-15.monsterpool.Caw Egg+Chest+Log+Thorn+Bandit+Blind+Carrier+Dragon Egg+Gnoll+Snake+Warchief+Alpha+Basilisk+Caw+Chomp+Cyclops+Demon+Ghost+Hydra+Ogre+Slate+Spiker+Troll.part.1.mn.Lair.doc.Floors 13 thru 15  
Pit:  
17-19.monsterpool.Shade+Sniper+Wisp+Barrel+Dragon Egg+Fanatic+Golem+Z0mbie+Zombie+Banshee+Basilisk+Chomp+Demon+Hydra+Spiker+Troll+Wizz.part.1.mn.Pit.doc.Floors 17-19  
All zones:  
1-3.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&5-7.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&9-11.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&13-15.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&17-19.monsterpool.Bee.part.0&1-3.monsterpool.Archer+Bee+Chest+Log+Rat+Seed+Thorn+Goblin+Grandma+Wolf+Boar.part.1&5-7.monsterpool.Chest+Illusion+Shade+Bandit+Blind+Barrel+Dragon Egg+Fountain+Goblin+Golem+Militia+Quartz+Snake+Sudul+Warchief+Cyclops+Ogre+Slate+Slimer.part.1&9-11.monsterpool.Bones+Chest+Grave+Illusion+Imp+Shade+Sniper+Carrier+Fanatic+Fountain+Golem+Militia+Quartz+Sudul+Z0mbie+Zombie+Banshee+Ghost+Slimer+Spiker+Wizz.part.1&13-15.monsterpool.Caw Egg+Chest+Log+Thorn+Bandit+Blind+Carrier+Dragon Egg+Gnoll+Snake+Warchief+Alpha+Basilisk+Caw+Chomp+Cyclops+Demon+Ghost+Hydra+Ogre+Slate+Spiker+Troll.part.1&17-19.monsterpool.Shade+Sniper+Wisp+Barrel+Dragon Egg+Fanatic+Golem+Z0mbie+Zombie+Banshee+Basilisk+Chomp+Demon+Hydra+Spiker+Troll+Wizz.part.1.mn.Basegame Enemies

### .Bal.

.bal. can be used to help balance your monsters in fight generation. It basically tells the game ‘hey this monsters is as strong as X monster’. For example  
	Dragon.bal.rat  
Tell the game that this dragon is as strong as a rat. So if you add that to a monsterpool you’d have something like 3 Dragon.bal.rats in fight 1\.  
I will work on figuring out the exact ‘points’ of difficulty each monster has later, for now you can use fight mode to get a rough approximation of how difficult the game thinks each monster is.   
For example the game thinks 2 Cyclops is a fight 13 fight, but 2 Banshee is a fight 15/17 fight. That means that .bal.banshee will make the game think your monster is tougher than .bal.Cyclops  
Generally you should just find a monster that is ‘about’ as tough, and give it a bal of that. For example if we think our Test rat is as strong as a goblin, we could add it to the first 3 fights by doing  
	1-3.monsterpool.((rat.bal.goblin).n.Test).part.1

I recommend using parenthesis around the monster.bal.monster because the game might apply the items, names, etc to the monster you are balancing around instead of yours

# To do 3.1 that should be documented

Hi, I will eventually update this guide with a more formatted and ‘professional’ explanation of these, but for now i just kinda wanna have fun, experiment, and make stuff.  
Still, heres my bullet points for now, if it helps you figure them out for yourself\!

* Hero.gift.modifier  
  * Gives modifier on hero pick  
* Facade  
* Enchant.modifier sides  
  * mage.i.mid.enchant.summon.bones  
* Cast sides  
  * cast.sthief.abilitydata.mage  
* Sticker sides  
  * mage.i.mid.sticker.shortsword  
* Summoning real  
  * mage.i.right5.left.hat.(egg.clumsy)\#blindfold  
* Replica.hero making mirror mask work  
* Hero.speech  
  * thief.speech.test1:test2:test3  
* Spellcasting update using new sides like summons and sticker  
* .doc. Update  
* Orb.ability  
  * orb.sthief.abilitydata.mage  
* ea.sthief.abilitydata.mage  
* Onhitdata.hero  
* Triggerhpdata.hero  
* Use hats of big enemies for enemy animation  
* Togpip  
  * Takes pips from left side  
* Togkey  
  * Takes keywords from left side  
* Togtime  
  * Toggle duration on stuff like enchanter  
* Togtarg  
  * Take targeting from left side  
* Togfri  
  * Toggle friendliness  
* Togvis  
  * Takes the visual effect (like fire from calderas 3 dmg stick) from left side  
* Togres  
  * Take restrictions from left side  
* Togeven  
  * No idea  
* Togeft  
  * Takes effect from left side

# 

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>

[image2]: <data:image/png;base64,>
